showcase_shot_visual: Your document as formatted text, math, and figures
showcase_shot_source: The same file in the source editor
showcase_shot_preview: Live PDF preview with a Problems list of compile errors
showcase_shot_references: A reference manager for your .bib files
home_title: Texpile - a local, offline LaTeX editor
home_meta_description: Texpile is a free, open source LaTeX editor. Edit your .tex files visually, keep byte-perfect output, compile with your own toolchain, and never leave your machine.
home_meta_keywords: LaTeX editor, visual LaTeX editor, WYSIWYG LaTeX editor, offline LaTeX editor, local LaTeX editor, open source LaTeX, LaTeX editor for Windows, LaTeX editor for macOS, LaTeX editor for Linux, .tex editor
home_social_description: Edit .tex files visually with byte-perfect saves, built-in git, and local compilation. Free and open source.
home_jsonld_feature_1: Visual and source LaTeX editing, kept in sync
hero_heading: A local, offline, open source LaTeX editor
hero_body: Texpile edits .tex files visually or in source, with a live preview compiled by your own LaTeX as you type. It runs entirely on your machine, and what it saves is plain LaTeX. No accounts, no cloud.
live_preview_body: The page updates as you type, compiled by your own LaTeX. No manual compile. Math, TikZ, and pgfplots figures render live.
live_preview_video_aria: Live preview compiling as you type
visual_editing_body: The editor parses your .tex source, renders what it can, and leaves the rest as plain LaTeX.
collab_body: Share a folder and edit it together in real time. Others join with a code from the home screen and work alongside you, in the visual editor or in source.
collab_point_1: Join with a code, no sign up
collab_point_2: End to end encrypted; the relay only forwards data it cannot read
collab_point_3: See each other's cursors and selections as you type
collab_point_4: You run the compile with your own toolchain; guests see the PDF
editing_point_1: Type @ to reference any equation, figure, table, or citation.
editing_point_2: Equations render inline and keep their numbering.
editing_point_3: Tables support multirow and multicolumn, edited visually.
editing_point_4: Spell check skips your commands and math.
intellisense_body: Texpile statically parses your whole project to produce intellisense.
intellisense_shot_alt: Source editor completing \cite with bibliography keys and titles
intellisense_point_1: Autocomplete for commands, environments, \ref labels, and \cite keys.
intellisense_point_3: References update automatically when you rename or move a file.
intellisense_point_4: Your own \newcommand macros are picked up.
diagnostics_body: Texpile reads the compile log and the bibliography log into a Problems panel. Click a problem to jump to its line.
diagnostics_shot_alt: Problems panel showing a compile error with a plain-language explanation, followed by warnings
feature_synctex_body: Click in the PDF to jump to the source line, and back.
feature_terminal_body: A real shell for latexmk or any compile command.
feature_history_body: Stage, commit, and diff side-by-side against the last commit.
feature_sync_body: Switching keeps scroll, cursor, and undo history.
feature_multifile_body: Fragments parse with the main file's macros.
feature_math_body: Equations preview live in the source editor.
faq_a_free: Yes. Texpile is free, and the source code is published on GitHub under AGPL-3.0.
faq_a_files: On your disk, as plain .tex files. There is no database and no proprietary format: what you save is exactly the LaTeX you would write by hand, and you can stop using the app at any time without losing anything.
faq_q_internet: Does it need an internet connection?
faq_a_internet: No. The editor, spell check, compiling, and PDF preview all run locally. Nothing leaves your machine unless you start a shared session, which is end to end encrypted.
faq_q_collab: Can I work with other people?
faq_a_collab: Yes, this is experimental. Share a folder for real time editing, and others join with a code. It is end to end encrypted, so the relay server only forwards data it cannot read.
faq_a_rewrite: No. The importer preserves every construct it does not understand as raw LaTeX, and saving a document you have not touched reproduces the file byte for byte. Only the specific blocks you edit are regenerated.
faq_a_latex_installed: For compiling PDFs, yes: bring your own TeX distribution (TeX Live, MiKTeX, or MacTeX) and Texpile runs it for you. Editing works without one.
faq_q_electron: Why is it built on Electron?
faq_a_electron: Texpile started as a web app, and the desktop version is a fork of it. The editor is built on ProseMirror and CodeMirror, which both rely on the browser feature contenteditable. That behavior differs between browser engines, and the web version needed many engine-specific fixes for cursor bugs, most of them on Safari. Electron bundles a fixed version of Chromium, so the editor targets one engine on every platform instead of three. Tauri is lighter, but it uses whatever WebView the operating system provides (WebKit on macOS), which reintroduces the cross-engine differences Electron avoids.
ps_body: Texpile is pronounced with the tex from text (tex-pile). The name is a blend of compiling LaTeX and transpiling it. We write it Texpile, not TeXpile.
dl_meta_description: Download Texpile, the free offline LaTeX editor, for Windows, macOS, and Linux. No account required.
dl_meta_keywords: download Texpile, LaTeX editor download, LaTeX editor for Windows, LaTeX editor for macOS, LaTeX editor for Linux
